title: Values, Types, Coercion, and Equality · Mastery Check
summary: Normalize prices, quantities, identifiers, optional fields, and validity signals from a mixed service-data feed. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a value-normalization and validation pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish undefined, null, Boolean, Number, BigInt, String, Symbol, and object values by behavior and use.
Use typeof, Array.isArray, instanceof, and explicit predicates while accounting for their boundaries.
Predict implicit coercion and prefer explicit conversion at external data boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Normalize service inputs [" 4 ", 4, null, {value:4}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use bigint and symbol only where their arithmetic, serialization, identity, and interoperability limits fit.

content.text: Normalize service inputs [" 4 ", 4, null, {value:4}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Bigint Symbol symptom.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ECMAScript 2026 Language Specification — Controls runtime semantics, types, expressions, execution contexts, jobs, modules, and language accuracy.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Normalize service inputs [" 4 ", 4, null, {value:4}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: BigInt can mix directly with Number in any arithmetic expression.

stimulus.title: Bigint Symbol failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: BigInt can mix directly with Number in any arithmetic expression.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use BigInt and Symbol only where their arithmetic, serialization, identity, and interoperability limits fit.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Bigint Symbol: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Normalize service inputs [" 2 ", 2, null, {value:2}] without implicit-coercion surprises. Record typeof, equality, parsed value, and the exact branch that rejects the object case.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use BigInt and Symbol only where their arithmetic, serialization, identity, and interoperability limits fit.
